surface.name: Get started with Algolia, Build your first search experience
surface.selection_basis: The official Get started page's Start building section points to Build your first search experience with sample data and React InstantSearch, and links that quickstart as the next document. Build with AI agents, concepts, libraries, integrations, and product-specific routes are alternatives rather than the selected general search quickstart.

alternatives_considered.name: Build with AI agents
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The general Get started page separates this as an AI-agent path, while Start building directly names the first search experience with sample data and React InstantSearch.

alternatives_considered.name: Algolia CLI getting started
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This teaches resource management from the CLI, while the general Get started page selects the React InstantSearch quickstart for the first search experience.

alternatives_considered.name: Shopify quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is specific to the Shopify integration rather than the general developer route.

entry_point.developer_intent: Build a first working end-to-end Algolia search experience with sample product data and React InstantSearch.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has a local command line and can create and edit JavaScript, TypeScript, CSS, and environment files.
The developer can create or access an Algolia account and open its dashboard.
The developer can run a local Vite development server and open localhost:5173 in a browser.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: You now have a working end-to-end search
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A local React InstantSearch app queries the new `quickstart-products` index, displays product results, updates the grid as the developer types, filters by product type, paginates, and highlights matching text.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: At `http://localhost:5173`, the browser shows a product grid, product-type filter, and search box; typing updates results and highlights matches. The guide then declares a working end-to-end search.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: After the Start the app instructions and browser exercise, the quickstart states, `You now have a working end-to-end search.`

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Build the search UI section ends by starting and exercising the app, then declares the end-to-end search working. Improve relevance, add features, collect events, and replace sample data follow that terminal.

prerequisites.requirement: An Algolia account, created for free during the path if necessary.
An Algolia application on a plan that permits the sample index and requests. The free Build plan requires no credit card and includes 1 million records and 10,000 search requests per month.
Node.js 20.19 or later, with npm available for the documented commands.
The selected Algolia application's Application ID, Write API key, and Search API key from the dashboard API Keys page.
Network access for npm installation, fetching Algolia's sample apparel dataset, indexing it, and querying the new index.

primary_path.action: Open the official Get started with Algolia page.
details: The page presents Start building, Build with AI agents, core concepts, and implementation stages.
primary_path.success_signal: Build your first search experience with sample data and React InstantSearch is visible under Start building.

primary_path.action: Open Build your first search experience.
details: The quickstart sets up a React app, indexes sample data, and builds a search interface.
primary_path.success_signal: The complete quickstart instructions are visible.

primary_path.action: If no Algolia account exists, follow Create one for free to the official signup page.
details: The page displays Google, GitHub, Vercel, and Work Email signup options and states that no credit card is required.
primary_path.output: The Get started for free signup page.
primary_path.success_signal: The documented identity options are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: JavaScript must be enabled to sign up.

primary_path.action: Choose Google, GitHub, Vercel, or Work Email signup.
primary_path.output: The selected identity or work-email flow begins.
primary_path.success_signal: The signup flow proceeds to account creation.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The public signup page does not document downstream OAuth screens.

primary_path.action: For Work Email signup, enter a work email and a password of at least eight characters, accept the listed terms and privacy notice, then select Sign up.
details: Acceptance covers the Terms of Service, Build Plan Terms, Service Limits, and privacy notice.
primary_path.input: Work email, qualifying password, and terms acceptance.
primary_path.output: The signup form is submitted; the next public state is not documented.
primary_path.success_signal: The Sign up action has been selected after the documented form requirements are satisfied; the next state remains unresolved.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The form blocks continuation until terms are accepted. Public content does not expose any email-verification or post-submit steps.

primary_path.action: Select the Algolia application whose credentials will be used by the quickstart.
details: If no application exists, open Settings, select Applications, click Create Application, choose a name and plan, complete billing if necessary, and choose a cluster region.
primary_path.input: An application accessible to the account, or the documented application-creation choices.
primary_path.output: An active application context in the dashboard.
primary_path.success_signal: The application's API Keys page can be opened.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The quickstart assumes an application but does not document whether signup creates one automatically.

primary_path.action: Create the React and TypeScript Vite project, then enter its directory.
primary_path.input: `npm create vite@latest algolia-quickstart-products -- --template react-ts --no-interactive` followed by `cd algolia-quickstart-products`
primary_path.output: A local Vite React TypeScript project in the current working directory.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The quickstart does not document recovery for failed Vite creation.

primary_path.action: Install the indexing, search UI, CSS, TypeScript runner, and spinner dependencies.
primary_path.input: `npm install algoliasearch instantsearch.css react-instantsearch ora tsx`
primary_path.output: The five packages and their dependencies are installed.
primary_path.success_signal: npm completes and the packages are available to the project.

primary_path.action: Open the application's API Keys page and copy the Application ID, Write API key, and Search API key.
details: The Write API key must remain secret; the search-only key is intended for frontend search use.
primary_path.output: Three credential values for local configuration.
primary_path.success_signal: Application ID, Write API key, and Search API key have been copied.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: API access requires a valid key.

primary_path.action: Create `.env.local` and assign the copied credentials to the documented variables.
details: Do not commit `.env.local` or expose the Write API key in the app.
primary_path.input: The Application ID, Write API key, and Search API key.
primary_path.output: Separate indexing and browser-search credentials in the Vite project.
primary_path.success_signal: All three environment variables have non-empty values.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The indexing script reports explicit missing-variable errors for an absent Application ID or Write API key.

primary_path.action: Create the indexing-script directory and file.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The combined command stops before `touch` if the directory already exists; the quickstart does not document that branch.

primary_path.action: Copy the quickstart's complete `scripts/indexing.ts` code into the file.
details: The script loads `.env.local`, creates an Algolia client, fetches the official apparel sample dataset, writes objects to `quickstart-products`, configures `product_type` for faceting, and waits for asynchronous save and settings tasks.
primary_path.output: The documented `indexProducts` implementation with status and error messages.
primary_path.success_signal: The complete supplied implementation is present.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The script throws for a missing Application ID, missing Write API key, or non-success dataset response. Its catch path prints `Indexing failed.` and sets a failing exit code.

primary_path.output: An `index:products` npm script pointing to the TypeScript indexing file.

details: The script fetches sample products, creates `quickstart-products`, uploads records, configures the `product_type` facet, and waits for Algolia's asynchronous tasks.
primary_path.output: An indexed and configured sample product catalog.
primary_path.success_signal: The terminal prints `Successfully indexed products.`; the index can be explored in the dashboard and the data is ready for search.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A failed path prints `Indexing failed.` and the underlying error. Build-plan limits include 1 GB per application and index, 10 indices, and a 10 KB maximum record size.

primary_path.action: Replace `src/App.tsx` with the complete React InstantSearch code supplied by the quickstart.
details: The app initializes the lite search client with the Application ID and Search API key, targets `quickstart-products`, and renders the documented search widgets.
primary_path.output: The documented search client and UI implementation.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The browser app depends on both `VITE_` variables and the previously created index; no separate runtime recovery is documented.

primary_path.action: Replace `src/App.css` with the complete CSS supplied by the quickstart.
details: The CSS provides the documented grid, filter, card, pagination, and responsive styling.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The guide says the styles affect presentation rather than search behavior.

primary_path.output: A local server exposing the app at `http://localhost:5173`.
primary_path.success_signal: The local URL can be opened in a browser.

primary_path.action: Open `http://localhost:5173` and enter a search query.
details: The product grid should update while typing, matching text should be highlighted, and product-type filtering and pagination should be present.
primary_path.success_signal: SearchBox updates results and pagination, product-type faceting works, Highlight and Snippet expose matches, and the guide declares a working end-to-end search.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No query-failure recovery is documented in the quickstart.

branches.condition: The developer already has an Algolia account
branches.path: Log in instead of creating a new account, then select an accessible application in the dashboard.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Skips signup but retains the dashboard-credential and React quickstart path.

branches.path: Use Google, GitHub, Vercel, or Work Email. Public content fully exposes the Work Email form and terms gate, but not downstream OAuth screens.
branches.effect_on_first_success: All routes are intended to create the account, but only Work Email can be fully reconstructed from public official content.

branches.condition: No Algolia application is available after signup
branches.path: Open Settings, select Applications, click Create Application, choose an application name and plan, complete billing if necessary, and select a cluster region.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Creates the application needed to expose an Application ID and API keys. The documented Build plan requires no credit card to get started.

friction_gates.description: An Algolia account and JavaScript-enabled browser are required.
Work Email signup requires acceptance of the Terms of Service, Build Plan Terms, Service Limits, and privacy notice.
The quickstart requires an application but does not establish whether signup creates one. Manual creation adds plan, billing-if-needed, and cluster-region choices.
The route requires Node.js 20.19 or later and five npm packages.
Three dashboard values must be copied and separated correctly between indexing and frontend use. The Write API key must not be committed or exposed.
The developer must populate a TypeScript indexing script, then replace a React component and a large CSS file with supplied source.
Algolia indexing and settings operations are asynchronous; the official script waits for both before reporting success.

The indexing script reports missing Application ID, missing Write API key, failed sample-dataset response, and caught indexing failures, but the guide provides no procedural recovery beyond correcting the cause and rerunning.

sources.title: Build your first search experience
sections_used: Set up your app and index sample data

sources.title: Pricing
sections_used: Pricing FAQs: Is there a free plan?
Who is the Algolia Build plan intended for?

sources.title: Algolia service limits
evidence_supported: Build-plan application, index, count, and record-size limits

sources.title: How do I create an application?
sections_used: Create a new application directly from your Algolia dashboard
evidence_supported: Name, plan, billing-if-needed, and cluster-region choices

uncertainties.question: Does a newly created Algolia account automatically receive its first application, or must the developer create one before opening API Keys?
uncertainties.impact: The new-user journey may include application name, plan, billing, and region selection before credentials can be copied.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The quickstart assumes an application. The public signup page does not document its post-submit state. An official help article documents manual application creation but does not say when a new account needs it.

uncertainties.question: What post-submit verification applies to Work Email signup, and what screens appear in each OAuth signup route?
uncertainties.impact: Additional account actions or waits may precede dashboard access.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Public official content exposes the identity choices, Work Email fields, password minimum, terms gate, and Sign up action, but not authenticated or email-delivery states.

excluded_after_success: Improving relevance by changing searchable attributes and custom ranking.
Adding search stats, active filters, menus, numerical filters, or other UI features.
Collecting view, filter-click, and hit-click events.
Replacing the sample catalog with production data.
Production access controls, security review, scale planning, analytics, and A/B testing.
